theme_notes: Touchstones: US-Iran 60-day nuclear deal roadmap (sanctions lifted, $300B reconstruction on the table), Keir Starmer's resignation with Andy Burnham as likely 7th PM in 10 years, FIFA World Cup 2026 ongoing across North America, Toy Story 5 in theaters (Tom Hanks back, screen-addiction plot), and France under extreme heatwave.
settled: You're balanced. More than can be said for the Middle East right now.
Clean. Even. Keir Starmer's legacy isn't, but yours is.
Settled up. France is on fire, the UK is leaderless, and somehow you two are fine.
youOwe: Less than a World Cup beer. Legally this doesn't even count as debt.
Iran gave up more than forty bucks to get sanctions lifted. Think about that.
Not enough to stress over. Cry about Toy Story 5 instead.
Bar tab money for a single World Cup match. You're better than this.
Enough to fund Iran's 60-day peace roadmap. Not enough to fund your dignity.
Britain's had seven prime ministers. You've had this debt the whole time.
France is baking in 40-degree heat. Your partner is baking in resentment. Same energy.
Keir Starmer resigned with more grace than you're handling this.
Woody came back for Toy Story 5. Your wallet is still lost under the couch.
Iran got sanctions lifted for less. What exactly did YOU offer?
That's more than Keir Starmer's pension. Which is a sentence that now means something.
The US Open ended with a clear winner. This has not.
Iran agreed to UN inspections faster than you're agreeing to pay this.
Andy Burnham inherits Britain with less baggage than your account right now.
The World Cup has group stages, knockout rounds, a final. This debt has none.
The Iran reconstruction fund is $300 billion. Scale accordingly.
Britain's had seven PMs in ten years. This balance outlasted all of them.
Tom Hanks came back for Toy Story 5. Your bank account is still in the incinerator.
theyOwe: Thirty bucks. The Iran deal took 60 days to sketch. This has taken longer.
They owe you coffee money. The World Cup is on and you're here tracking receipts.
Less than a Toy Story 5 ticket. Both leave you quietly devastated.
Not a lot. But France isn't that hot either. Wait. France is absolutely on fire.
The US Open just crowned a champion. Your partner can't close a Venmo request.
One fifty. Andy Burnham's first press conference had less awkward silence than this.
Enough for a World Cup group-stage seat. You're watching from home anyway.
France is on fire and so are you. Three hundred dollars of righteous, documented anger.
Toy Story 5 is in theaters. You're both crying, but only one of you has an excuse.
The US and Iran closed a deal faster than your partner closes a tab.
The UK's on its seventh PM. You're owed eight hundred. Both numbers feel too high.
The US Open champion went home with hardware. You get this invoice.
Iran's sanctions are lifting. Your debt ceiling isn't.
The World Cup has a champion in waiting. Your invoice has no such deadline.
Andy Burnham inherited a broken country. You inherited a broken IOU.
For this much, Iran would negotiate with you directly. And they'd probably win.
France on fire. UK without a PM. Five grand owed. Pick your disaster.
Buzz Lightyear said to infinity and beyond. This debt agreed wholeheartedly.
